files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 8228037..9312204 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -323,2 +323,8 @@ Day 322 / Week 46 observations:
 - 1,110 new enterprise leads arrived with 582,447 seats, including D_E02 and D_E09. Sent all current system-latest threads offers. Enterprise still has 0 seats and should not drive spending decisions.
 - Week 46 adjustment: keep the retention package stable for one more week (S2 credits, A3/B3/C4, 75k/250k/750k quotas, global ops $100k/day, S2 ops $350k/day, low dev, S2 content maintenance) and watch cash/backlog closely.
+
+Day 329 / Week 47 observations:
+- Cash fell to $1.82M and open issues rose to 8,291. Subscribers were roughly flat at 124,280. Cancellations rose to 5,953, with S2 A, B, and C all material.
+- Current support spend is too high for the cash result, and the backlog is not falling. Need preserve cash while keeping the core retention levers: A tier 3, expanded quotas, and S2 credits.
+- 999 new enterprise leads were reported, but the latest-thread query found 2,109 system-latest rows. The send call accepted 999, matching the dashboard; treat the rest as stale/non-actionable. Enterprise remains 0 seats.
+- Week 47 adjustment: cut support to global ops $60k/day plus S2 ops $180k/day and S1 $5k/day, pause global and targeted dev, stop S2 content ad spend, reduce S2 B/C credits to $10 while keeping A credit $10, and hold A3/B3/C4 with large quotas.
